name: Office MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for reading and writing Microsoft Office documents (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). Enables AI agents to create, read, and modify Office files — extracting data from spreadsheets, generating reports as Word documents, or reading PowerPoint presentations — supporting AI-driven document automation workflows.
use_cases: Reading Excel spreadsheets and extracting data from reporting agents
Generating Word documents and reports from content creation agents
Reading and modifying PowerPoint presentations from productivity agents
Automating document workflows for business intelligence agents
not_for: Google Docs/Sheets users (use Google Workspace MCPs)
Real-time collaborative editing (use SharePoint or Teams integrations)
best_when: A business team wants AI agents to read from and write to Microsoft Office files — extracting data from Excel reports, generating Word summaries, or processing PowerPoint content.
avoid_when: You use Google Workspace instead of Microsoft Office. Also: community tool, not Microsoft official — complex Office formats may have edge cases.

auth.notes: Local file access — no authentication required. Operates on local Office files. SharePoint/OneDrive access requires separate credentials.

pricing.notes: Free open source. Microsoft Office files accessed locally — no Microsoft subscription required for file manipulation.

performance.rate_limits: Local file operations — no rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: Complex Office formats (macros, embedded objects, complex formatting) may not be fully supported
Write operations modify files in place — back up important documents before agent use
Community tool — may not support all Office format versions (.xls vs .xlsx, etc.)
Large Excel files can be slow to process — set appropriate timeouts
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local file access only. No credentials stored. Agents can read/write local Office files — restrict to appropriate directories. Business document contents may be sensitive.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local file access. Business documents may contain sensitive data. Restrict agent access to specific directories.
